goal_results.evidence: arr-monitor.py:87-93 — IGNORE_EXTENSIONS set contains '.exe' and '.msi' alongside existing entries including '.nfo'. Test G1 returns FULL.

arr-monitor.py:1002 — '-A'/'--add-new-processes' registered in argparse with help text. arr-monitor.py:802-806 — run_monitor signature includes add_new_processes param. arr-monitor.py:1080 — call site passes args.add_new_processes. arr-monitor.py:858-875 — monitoring loop calls find_arr_processes() and extends pid_list with new PIDs when flag is active. Tests G2-G5 all pass.

arr-monitor.py:1007-1009 — 'if args.add_new_processes and not args.all: print("Error: --add-new-processes requires --all"); return 1'. Test G6 passes: running with --add-new-processes alone exits non-zero with message mentioning --all.
arr-monitor.py:1002 — '-A' short option registered for --add-new-processes. arr-monitor.py:1000 — '-a' short option registered for --all.

judge_notes: All 4 rubric goals met. test.sh confirms perfect score (1.000) with all 6 inner gates passing (G1=FULL, G2=1, G3=1, G4=FULL, G5=FULL, G6=1). The patch correctly merges .exe/.msi into IGNORE_EXTENSIONS, implements the --add-new-processes flag end-to-end (argparse, run_monitor signature, call site, runtime rescan logic), adds validation requiring --all, and registers short options -A and -a. Score = 0.4 + 0.4 + 0.1 + 0.1 = 1.00.
